steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The text has an independent clause (who served in...Congress) followed by a list of examples (Ralph Harold Metcalfe...Alton Waldon Jr.). A colon introduces a list or elaboration.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
The structure: a complete clause ("...served in Congress") followed by a list of two names that specify which Black Americans. The rule: a colon introduces an explanation or list that elaborates on what came before.
  • Choice A — "Congress:": colon correctly introduces the list of names that specify the people served in Congress. ✓
  • Choice B — "Congress;": semicolon requires an independent clause on the right side, but "Ralph Harold Metcalfe... and Alton R. Waldon Jr..." is a list of names, not a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — "Congress.": period ends the sentence, leaving the name list as a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — "Congress": no punctuation creates a fused construction where the names appear to be the direct object of "served in," which is grammatically incoherent. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
